mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces the relative attractiveness of cash/bond substitutes, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a 0.3 percentage point increase over the trailing 2 months, it signals a deteriorating labor market that raises recession probability and triggers a short-horizon risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline (p > 0.05) in more than 55% of triggered instances over the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based consumer and construction demand strength that compounds into corporate earnings growth over a full quarter, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone captures the full demand-side signal because housing leads durable investment while retail sales reflects contemporaneous consumption.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST+RSAFS acceleration signal produces 60-day forward returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-to-mid cycle industrial expansion, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strengthening manufacturing supply capacity and corporate output, which forward-predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship is regime-conditional because in late-cycle or overheating regimes, rising INDPRO can instead signal impending capacity-driven inflation pressure and monetary tightening risk that offsets the positive earnings channel.
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle industrial expansion with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods with FEDFUNDS below its trailing 12-month median, invalidating the regime-conditional distinction.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects real-time consumer spending momentum that is rapidly incorporated into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days on a persistent, always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms genuine labor market tightening rather than a statistical artifact of falling labor force participation, and this combined signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days because it validates sustainable consumption capacity; PAYEMS growth alone can be misleading when UNRATE rises concurrently due to labor force re-entry effects.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns conditional on joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down signal are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across the walk-forward evaluation sample.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate rises above a 4.5% threshold, restrictive monetary policy begins to materially compress equity valuation multiples and slow credit-financed corporate investment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rate
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime following a GDP contraction, an uptick in industrial production (INDPRO) alongside quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth signals the early stage of a supply-side rebound that markets have not yet fully priced, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as high-frequency confirmation of the recovery narrative arrives.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery windows, INDPRO month-over-month gains fail to precede positive 5-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.
